instant.evidence: Inert default blueprint config; it injects no architecture context and has no runtime behavior of its own.

natural.evidence: Static configuration object, not an interactive agent.

permitted.evidence: No agent behavior; governance applies at the pipeline that consumes the blueprint, not at the blueprint itself.

adaptive.evidence: Static no-op default; nothing to learn.

contextual.evidence: Empty default: forced_depth_mode null, no feature seeds, no architecture context injected.

transparent.evidence: Inert plain-config default; fully inspectable as committed JSON.

governance.evidence: Validated against blueprint.schema.json and consumed only inside the approval-gated build pipeline.

observability.evidence: Inert static config; no runtime signals to observe.

lexicon.evidence: Uses the canonical blueprint schema vocabulary (id/label/forced_depth_mode/feature_seeds).

solid.evidence: Static, schema-validated, deterministic default with no side effects.

layers.name: Multi-Agent Orchestration
layers.how: Selectable architecture preset feeding the build pipeline (here: the no-op default).

notes: The 'standard' blueprint is an intentionally empty default. INPACT is largely n_a because the artifact prescribes no AI behavior; the agents a project later builds under this blueprint each require their own attestation.
